<<Economic Stress and Substance Abuse Analysis>>
The maximum number of substance abuse incidents occurs at an economic stress index of 2, yielding 13 incidents per 1000 people. To keep incidents below 20, the community must maintain a stress index between approximately 0.22 and 3.78. This analysis is as critical for human well-being as a pitcher is for baseball or water is for a western town. Ensuring these limits are met is essential for the health of the population.
